investigation.scope: First FMIS interoperability investigation for crop-protection scouting to audit reconstruction.
investigation.first_workflow_slice: crop-protection scouting -> recommendation -> prescription/work order -> contractor or machine application -> as-applied record -> correction/dispute -> audit reconstruction
investigation.recommended_next_action: Use the live BigQuery KIS/FMIs-like schema as the first adapter discovery surface, but collect raw export/API payload samples, correction history examples, authority records, and original payload references before starting a compliance-grade mapping spike.

systems.name: LoginEKO BigQuery data layer
systems.role_in_workflow: Read-only analytical warehouse exposing FMIS-like operational marts and source-shaped tables for fields, crop zones, scouting, planned operations, actual operations, materials, equipment, storage, inventory, and traceability.

evidence.location: bq ls and INFORMATION_SCHEMA live checks
evidence.note: Live access showed 162 prod-style datasets, including prod__t_kis__* and prod__farming__* families.

systems.name: KIS scouting marts
systems.role_in_workflow: Scouting observations with observation time, WGS84 location, EPPO-linked target identity, severity, impacted area, image URLs, comments, field geometry, and observer person fields.

evidence.note: Schema exposes observation UUIDs, timestamps, point geometry, EPPO fields, severity, image URLs, JSON comments, field geometry and person fields.

systems.name: KIS operations marts
systems.role_in_workflow: Planned and actual field operations, including application operations, equipment, people, materials, rates, units, crop links, operation zones, statuses, and last-modified watermarks.

evidence.location: bq show schema and aggregate counts
evidence.note: Live counts showed 607 planned application operations and 409 completed actual application operations, plus aborted and in-progress actual application statuses.

systems.name: KIS field and crop-zone marts
systems.role_in_workflow: Field identity, field geometry versions, crop zones, crop effective dates, crop-zone operation intersections, and projection/reporting surfaces.

evidence.location: bq show schema and aggregate counts
evidence.note: Field schema exposes persistent field IDs, UUIDs, field origin, geometry version IDs, WGS84 geography, SCD effective times, area, source origin, change author, and changed timestamp.

systems.name: KIS material catalogue
systems.role_in_workflow: Local material/product catalogue with material identity, category, base unit, crop links, target rate, application unit, SKU bridge, organic flag, active/available flags, and modification timestamp.

evidence.note: Schema exposes material_id, material_uuid, name, category, base_unit, target_rate, target_rate_unit, conversion factor, SKU bridge, crops, crop varieties, is_organic, is_available, and is_active.

systems.name: KIS soil measurement surface
systems.role_in_workflow: Potential measurement/lab evidence surface for soil sampling geometry, sampling timestamp, analyte type, and numeric result.
systems.version_or_date: Table last modified 2026-05-13; live row count 0

evidence.location: bq show schema and aggregate count
evidence.note: Schema exists but live count was zero, so it proves a modeled surface but not usable pilot evidence.
125 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:05:23; includes field_uuid, field_geometry_uuid, origin fields, SCD effective times, WGS84 geometry, area, field_changed_at, and change_author.

4 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 09:26:00; max observation_time 2026-03-24 13:28:34.740+00; includes observation UUIDs, point geometry, EPPO identifiers, severity, impacted area, image URLs, comments, field geometry, and observer person fields.

1832 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:05:57; 607 planned APPLICATION rows; includes planned time window, planned coverage, status, vehicles, implements, persons, crops, materials, quantities, rates and units.

1124 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:05:57; 409 completed APPLICATION rows, 8 aborted APPLICATION rows, and 1 in-progress APPLICATION row; includes actual time window, coverage, fuel, active time, materials, persons, vehicles, crops, status, and last_modified_timestamp.
283 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:06:05; includes crop zone geometry, operation zone geometry, operation materials, planned people/equipment, crop effective dates and operation time window.

1999 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:06:25; includes crop zone geometry, operation zone geometry, intersection ratios, actual people/equipment/materials, operation status and last_modified_timestamp.

100 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 12:05:35; includes material UUID, category, base unit, target rate, application unit, SKU bridge, crop links, crop-variety links, organic flag, active flag and modification timestamp.

0 rows; table last modified 2026-05-13 09:25:11; schema includes sample ID, sample geometry, sampling date, measurement type and numeric value, but no live rows or lab method/accreditation fields were observed.

mappings.recommended_action: Use as field identity candidate input, but retain source origin and geometry version provenance rather than treating the mart row as OFARM truth.

missing_or_ambiguous_fields: CRS is described as WGS84 but no explicit source payload CRS object was inspected
mappings.recommended_action: Map geometry versions as extent snapshots with basis and quality caveats; use effective range for reconstruction.
Map as observation context only; do not imply recommendation, prescription, or treatment consequence from scouting rows.
Treat image URLs and comments as evidence references with caveats until asset custody and review metadata are supplied.

missing_or_ambiguous_fields: recommendation versus prescription versus work-order authority class
mappings.recommended_action: Map planned application operations as intent candidates only; require authority and source-payload evidence before compliance-grade prescription mapping.
Map as operation claim/as-applied evidence candidates; separate actual status from accepted OFARM consequence.

missing_or_ambiguous_fields: whether operation zone is planned, actual, or projected from another surface in every row
mappings.recommended_action: Use as a strong candidate for event-bound treatment extents, but preserve projected/intersection caveats and do not assign durable identities automatically.
Do not promote actual operation rows directly to accepted consequences; build a promotion gate that checks authority, source health, evidence sufficiency, and correction/dispute state.
Map local material identity as a local binding only; require regulatory/supply-chain bindings before compliance-grade crop-protection product identity.
Keep as modeled future measurement surface; request actual lab/report samples before mapping to accepted measurement evidence.
Build reconstruction traces only after original source payload references and correction lineage are available.

gaps.title: Raw payloads and original file/API references were not supplied
gaps.ofarm_impact: The warehouse schema can guide adapter design, but OFARM import cannot preserve source payload provenance, hashes, parser loss maps, or exact vendor semantics yet.
gaps.recommended_action: Collect representative redacted exports/API responses or source-row packets for the same field and operation chain.

gaps.title: Correction, supersession, and dispute history are not proven
gaps.ofarm_impact: Current rows and last-modified watermarks are not enough to reconstruct what was known before and after a correction or dispute.
gaps.recommended_action: Request before/after operation exports, audit tables, revision logs, deleted/superseded rows, and a real disputed or corrected operation example.

gaps.title: Authority and contractor/applicator evidence are incomplete
gaps.ofarm_impact: Person, vehicle, and implement fields identify participants, but do not prove legal authority, contractor delegation, license, or acceptance.
gaps.recommended_action: Request job sheets, delegation records, operator/applicator role evidence, contractor invoices, and acceptance/review decisions for one crop-protection operation.

gaps.title: Product identity is local-catalogue grade, not regulatory-grade
gaps.ofarm_impact: Local material names, SKUs, categories, base units, and rates are useful, but do not prove authorised product identity, active substance, label constraints, or jurisdiction.
gaps.recommended_action: Bind crop-protection materials to jurisdiction-scoped product authorisations, registrant/manufacturer data, active substances, GTIN/SKU where available, and label/rate constraints.

gaps.title: Projection/reporting surfaces could be mistaken for truth
gaps.ofarm_impact: Tables with projected/intersection names and current mart rows are useful reconstruction aids, but OFARM requires assertion/history-first truth and governed materialization.
gaps.recommended_action: Label projected marts as derived evidence and require trace back to source assertions before promotion or audit output.

gaps.title: Soil/lab measurement surface has schema but no live evidence rows
gaps.ofarm_impact: MeasurementEvidence mapping cannot be proven from an empty table and lacks lab method, accreditation, units, uncertainty, and custody fields.
gaps.recommended_action: Request actual lab report exports or rows with method, units, result time, lab identity, accreditation, and chain-of-custody metadata.

adapter_backlog.title: Build BigQuery KIS source inventory adapter
adapter_backlog.description: Create read-only discovery code that inventories selected prod__t_kis__ tables, captures row counts, last-modified times, schema hashes, join keys, and source-health warnings without exporting sensitive rows by default.

adapter_backlog.title: Map field and crop-zone geometries to Field and PartialExtent candidates
adapter_backlog.description: Use field_uuid, field_geometry_uuid, SCD effective ranges, WGS84 GEOGRAPHY, origin, change author, crop_zone_id, operation_zone_geometry, and intersection ratios to create event-bound extent candidates with explicit basis and quality caveats.

adapter_backlog.title: Separate planned intent from actual execution
adapter_backlog.description: Map planned operation rows to InterventionIntentPayload candidates and actual operation rows to ExecutionRecordPayload candidates while preventing direct promotion to accepted consequences.

adapter_backlog.title: Implement scouting observation importer
adapter_backlog.description: Map scouting UUID, observation time, point geometry, EPPO target fields, severity, impacted area, comments, image URLs, field and observer references to AgronomicObservationContext plus caveated EvidenceReference records.

adapter_backlog.title: Implement material identity binding enrichment
adapter_backlog.description: Preserve local material IDs, UUIDs, names, categories, units, target rates and SKU bridge, then add jurisdiction-scoped crop-protection product bindings before compliance use.

adapter_backlog.title: Design correction and dispute reconstruction probe
adapter_backlog.description: Identify audit tables or source event update streams that prove deleted, corrected, superseded, aborted, disputed, and late-synced records, then map them to history-preserving OFARM assertions.

risks.description: Derived BigQuery marts and projected crop-zone operation tables are convenient but may hide source loss, joins, filters, and correction history.
risks.mitigation: Treat them as mapping/discovery evidence only until source payload references, loss maps, and promotion traces are built.

risks.description: Last-modified timestamps do not prove immutable correction, supersession, or dispute history.
risks.mitigation: Require audit/revision examples and preserve old and new assertions rather than overwriting OFARM history.

risks.description: Person, vehicle, implement, and task links do not prove who had authority to recommend, prescribe, perform, review, or accept a crop-protection operation.
risks.mitigation: Add authority/delegation/job-sheet evidence before any compliance-grade prescription or accepted execution mapping.

risks.description: Material rates and quantities expose unit strings, but quantity-kind semantics and normalized-versus-original value preservation are not yet proven.
risks.mitigation: Store original value/unit text and add QUDT quantity-kind plus UCUM/unit-code binding before calculations or high-consequence output.

risks.description: Image URLs and comments are available for scouting observations, but binary custody, hashes, uploader, deletion/version audit, and review status are not proven.
risks.mitigation: Treat as caveated evidence references until an evidence asset manifest is supplied.

risks.description: Live tables expose person and field/farm identifiers; row-level extracts could reveal personal or commercially sensitive operational details.
risks.mitigation: Use redacted packets for review and keep raw row extraction scoped, authorized, and minimized.

evidence_requests.requested_material: One complete redacted crop-protection operation packet linking scouting observation, recommendation or prescription, planned work order, actual/as-applied record, and any accepted/reviewed outcome.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to prove join keys across observation, intent, execution, and promotion without guessing from table names.

evidence_requests.requested_material: Before/after correction or aborted/failed/partial application example, including audit/revision history and current row state.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to test OFARM correction, dispute, supersession, and reconstruction behavior.
acceptable_formats: API responses before and after correction

evidence_requests.requested_material: Authority and responsibility evidence for the same operation: recommender, prescriber, planned operator, actual operator, contractor if any, reviewer, and accepter.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to separate recommendation, prescription, planned operation, operation claim, and accepted consequence.

evidence_requests.requested_material: Original machine/controller/as-applied source for one application with geometry and material rate details.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to prove whether BigQuery actual operation rows preserve or derive as-applied facts.

evidence_requests.requested_material: Material/product identity evidence for crop-protection materials used in the sample operation.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to bind local materials to regulatory product identity, active substances, label constraints, units, and permitted rates.

evidence_requests.requested_material: Evidence asset manifest for scouting images and operation attachments.
evidence_requests.why_needed: Required to determine whether image URLs are merely references or custody-grade evidence.

evidence_requests.requested_material: Actual soil or lab report sample if lab/measurement workflows are in the pilot slice.
evidence_requests.why_needed: The soil measurement table exists but had zero rows; lab method, accreditation, units, uncertainty, and custody remain unproven.

unresolved_questions.question: Is the first implementation spike meant to use the prod__t_kis__* BigQuery marts as the primary FMIS source, or should they be treated only as derived analytical surfaces over a separate source API/database?
Which table or source object carries recommendation or prescription authority, as distinct from planned operation scheduling?
Where are corrections, cancellations, deleted rows, superseded geometry versions, disputes, and late contractor sync events stored?
Do operation_zone_geometry values come from machine as-applied logs, manually drawn operation zones, crop-zone intersections, or another derived process?
Which material categories and application_type values correspond to crop-protection products, and how are they bound to product authorizations or active substances?
Are scouting image URLs stable evidence references with upload metadata and deletion audit, or convenience links without custody guarantees?

source_list.note: Active OFARM context package extracted read-only into ofarm_fmis_investigation_2026-05-13/tmp_ofarm.
Used for standards role discipline and agronomic carrier expectations.

source_list.path_or_url: BigQuery project login-eko-data-layer, queried with billing project login-eko-samoa-sbx
source_list.note: Live BigQuery metadata, table schemas, table counts, row counts and last-modified checks.
Earlier local shadow assessment used only as a reference; it explicitly did not run live database queries.
Metadata package handoff used for starting context; live BigQuery checks supersede stale metadata where they differ.

notes: This is a partial live-discovery investigation. BigQuery proves relevant FMIS-like surfaces and enough fields to design a first adapter spike, but no raw source export bundle was present under input_fmis_materials and no row-level source packet was exported for this run. Do not treat current marts or projected tables as OFARM truth.
